rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system backed by Box, using the Box Swift SDK (BoxSdkGen).  Overview This backend requires the BoxSDK package. With the source distribution, enable the Box package trait and SPM resolves it automatically. With the binary distribution (XCFrameworks), add BoxSDK as a separate package dependency in your project. Box uses numeric IDs rather than paths to identify files and folders. This backend bridges the path-based CloudFileSystem protocol onto Box’s ID-based API by walking the folder hierarchy to resolve Ensembles paths (e.g. ensembleId/events/file.json) to Box folder and file IDs. Resolved IDs are cached in internal dictionaries to minimize API calls. The cache is invalidated when items are deleted or when not-found errors indicate stale entries. The app creates and configures a BoxClient with its own authentication and injects it at init time:  Authentication The Box Swift SDK supports multiple authentication methods. Create a BoxClient with the appropriate auth for your use case: Developer Token (testing only) Get a short-lived token from developer.box.com under your app’s configuration page. These tokens expire after 60 minutes.  OAuth 2.0 (production apps) Register your app at developer.box.com and configure an OAuth 2.0 redirect URI:  Client Credentials Grant (server-side) For server-to-server integration without user interaction:  Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. The BoxClient handles its own thread safety internally. Mutable state (caches, loggedIn flag) is accessed only from async methods that are serialized by the ensemble’s operation queue.
summary: A cloud file system backed by Box, using the Box Swift SDK (BoxSdkGen).
title: BoxCloudFileSystem

rawIndexableTextContent: A Box-based cloud backend for Ensembles. Overview EnsemblesBox provides BoxCloudFileSystem, an implementation of the Ensembles CloudFileSystem protocol that stores sync data on Box. It uses the official Box Swift SDK (BoxSdkGen) for all API operations. Box is widely used in enterprise environments and offers strong administrative controls, compliance features, and large storage quotas. The ID Problem Unlike path-based services (pCloud, WebDAV, S3), Box identifies every file and folder by a numeric ID. The Ensembles CloudFileSystem protocol uses string paths like ensembleId/events/file.json. BoxCloudFileSystem bridges this gap by walking the folder hierarchy to resolve paths to Box IDs, caching the mappings to minimize API calls. Authentication Box supports multiple authentication methods (OAuth 2.0, Developer Token, Client Credentials Grant, JWT). Your app creates and configures a BoxClient with the appropriate auth, then injects it:  Setup  Path Resolution and Caching When Ensembles requests an operation on path /ensembleId/events/file.json, the backend: Starts at the Box root folder (ID "0"). Lists the root folder to find ensembleId and gets its folder ID. Lists that folder to find events and gets its folder ID. Lists that folder to find file.json and gets its file ID. Each resolved ID is cached in an internal dictionary. Subsequent operations on the same path skip the API calls. The cache is invalidated when items are deleted or when not-found errors suggest staleness. Folder listings are paginated automatically (up to 1000 items per page) to handle large directories. Error Handling Box SDK errors are mapped to EnsembleError cases: Authentication failures (401, unauthorized) map to EnsembleError/authenticationFailure. Not-found errors (404) map to EnsembleError/fileAccessFailed. Conflict errors (409, item already exists) are handled internally with retry logic. All other errors map to EnsembleError/serverError. Dependencies This module depends on the Box iOS SDK v10+ (BoxSdkGen module). The SDK has zero external dependencies of its own.
summary: A Box-based cloud backend for Ensembles.
title: EnsemblesBox
